options.text: art needs no use — its uselessness is the point
art must serve political causes
only artists may look at art

exercises.explanation: La gratuité du beau, gloriously assumed: beauty's oldest defence.

exercises.prompt: Reformulating a creator's thought goes wrong in two ways:

options.text: flat paraphrase (idea minus its life) and overreading (your idea, their name)
writing too neatly and citing the date
using the present tense and quotation marks

exercises.explanation: Keep the tension, lose the pose — betrayal has exactly two flavours.

exercises.prompt: The moment the outsider is let in.
exercises.template: Le musée offre la {{b1}} — et pose ses conditions.
exercises.explanation: La consécration — official recognition. Not consumption, and ideally not consternation.

exercises.prompt: Who has funded the gesture since Maecenas.
exercises.template: Le {{b1}} finance le geste et signe le mur.
exercises.explanation: Le mécénat — patronage, from Maecenas to the foundation with naming rights.

exercises.prompt: Goya's war paintings serve mainly which function of art?

options.text: Témoigner — bearing witness to the era
Décorer — decorating the salon
Consoler — soothing the viewer

exercises.explanation: Art as the era's record: Goya testifies; the history textbook confirms.

exercises.template: L'école peut éveiller la {{b1}} que la maison n'a pas le temps de voir.
exercises.explanation: Éveiller la sensibilité — cultivated like a language, and just as transferable.

exercises.prompt: Match each function of art to its verb.
Put the words in order: "The wall was free; the ticket is not."
Listen to the curator. Which function is she actually defending?
exercises.transcript: Nous n'exposons pas pour plaire : cette salle dérange, et c'est précisément pour cela qu'elle existe.

options.text: Contester — art as challenge; disturbing IS the room's purpose
Décorer — pleasing the visitor's eye
Consoler — comforting the public

exercises.explanation: « Cette salle dérange, c'est pour cela qu'elle existe » — the avant-garde's job description.

exercises.prompt: Read this column on street art's museum debut and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Le pochoir qui valait trois nuits de garde à vue vaut désormais six gardiens de salle. En entrant au musée, le street art gagne la climatisation, l'assurance et le catalogue — et perd la rue, c'est-à-dire son adresse et une partie de son verbe. Consécration, applaudit le marché ; domestication, grince l'ancien complice du peintre, qui note que l'œuvre contestait jadis le mur même où on l'encadre. La fonction a-t-elle survécu au déménagement ? Le billet d'entrée, lui, se porte très bien.
exercises.passageEn: The stencil once worth three nights in custody is now worth six room attendants. By entering the museum, street art gains air conditioning, insurance and a catalogue — and loses the street, that is, its address and part of its verb. Consecration, applauds the market; domestication, grumbles the painter's old accomplice, who notes the work once protested the very wall it's now framed on. Did the function survive the move? The entrance ticket, for its part, is doing very well.

questions.prompt: What does street art lose by entering the museum, per the column?

options.text: The street itself — its address and part of its protest
Its colours, due to air conditioning
Nothing; the move is pure gain

questions.prompt: The closing line about the ticket is…

options.text: irony: whatever happened to the art's function, the business function thrives
a complaint that tickets are too cheap
proof the museum is failing

exercises.prompt: Against « il y a déjà trop de matières », the C2 defence of art class is:

options.text: refuse the false ledger — art trains the attention the other subjects will use
concede that art is a luxury for later
propose removing mathematics instead

exercises.explanation: Don't fight for a slot in the timetable; show the timetable depends on what art trains.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Le street art entre au musée : consécration ou domestication ?

options.text: Street art enters the museum: consecration or taming?
The wall was free; the ticket is not.

a work
the approach
to move

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.template: La {{b1}} du beau est sa plus vieille défense.
exercises.explanation: « La gratuité du beau est sa plus vieille défense. » — uselessness / freedom from purpose.
